cards.back: A supervised learning algorithm that uses a tree-like model to classify data by recursively partitioning the data into smaller subsets based on the values of the input features.
The topmost node in a decision tree, which represents the input feature(s) used to split the data into subsets.
A terminal node in a decision tree, which represents a predicted class label or a decision made based on the input features.
An attribute or variable used to split the data into subsets in a decision tree.
The direction or axis in which the data is split into subsets in a decision tree, e.g., by value, by range, etc.
